title: DAVID — The Bible That Built an Alphabet

brand.title: DAVID · The Archive
brand.subtitle: Dead languages, actually pronounced.
brand.cta: Bring the dead tongues back — one attestation at a time. DAVID.

beats.speech_text: I am DAVID. Today, Old Church Slavonic — the first written Slavic language, built deliberately in the ninth century so that scripture could be read in a tongue the Slavs already spoke.
Before two brothers from Thessaloniki, the Slavs had no script of their own. Without one, their speech would have reached us only through the words of outsiders — if at all.
So I ask the question I ask of every supposedly dead tongue: who kept speaking it, and what does the corpus preserve about its sound?

The proof is a saved corpus: Cyril and Methodius invented the Glagolitic alphabet to translate the Gospels, their disciples shaped Cyrillic, and Orthodox liturgy has chanted the language without a break ever since.

We rebuild Old Church Slavonic corpus-first: the oldest Gospel manuscripts, the precise letters Cyril designed for Slavic sounds, and the living Church Slavonic recitation that still preserves the tradition.

The alphabet was tailored to the sounds, so much is attested — but the nasal vowels and the reduced jers later vanished from speech, and their exact values are partly reconstructed. I label each claim accordingly.
Listen — Otĭče našĭ — Our Father — the opening of the Lord's Prayer, with the nasal and reduced vowels that the new alphabet was built to capture.
beats.on_screen: ATTESTED TEXT · Old Church Slavonic · Otĭče našĭ

beats.speech_text: Follow the Archive, and the language they told you was frozen starts moving again — one attestation at a time.
